commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b4f0b003223a1fcd8e5364a17a8e1d493b06eb85)
commands.review_summary:
The branch defers local-model lean filtering until after Code Mode or Tool Search catalog registration, keeps final visible filtering, adds focused tests, and updates local-model docs.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +83, Docs 0. Total +109 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than execution: current main filters `localModelLean` tools before catalog registration, so the three tools cannot enter the hidden catalog. The PR adds focused tests that encode the intended before-catalog and final-visible behavior.
commands.review_followup: - Human maintainer review should decide the lean-mode compatibility contract and require config-help alignment; automation cannot safely choose that policy.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lean tools behind catalog controls
target.body:
Summary:
- Keep `localModelLean` from removing heavyweight tools before Code Mode or Tool Search can place them behind compact catalog controls.
- Preserve the final visible lean-mode filter, so `browser`, `cron`, and `message` stay out of the model-visible schema when lean mode is on.
- Add focused coverage for pre-catalog vs final visible filtering and update local-model docs.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts` - local node wrapper, 1 file / 3 tests passed after rebase.
- AWS Crabbox remote proof: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_63a1164b95ec`, run `run_e8eeac6f021c`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts`, 1 file / 3 tests passed, exit `0`, cleanup `stopped=true`.
- `git diff --check $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main)..HEAD`.
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md`.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts`.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/local-model-lean-catalog.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts`.

Behavior addressed: lean local-model runs no longer lose policy-allowed `browser`, `cron`, and `message` capability before compact catalog controls can represent those tools cheaply.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_63a1164b95ec`, run `run_e8eeac6f021c`, branch head `5650401a6f0b879ba3b81aef751cc69dc37f916c`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `7708e8c7efc6308f1c693474a743a216baacf834`; live main advanced once after proof.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: checked the rebased diff, checked docs formatting for the touched docs, checked TypeScript format/lint for touched source, and ran the focused `local-model-lean-catalog` Vitest file locally and remotely through Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: diff check passed; docs formatter reported `Docs formatting clean (647 files)`; local and Crabbox Vitest both reported 1 file and 3 tests passed.
Observed result after fix: lean mode keeps heavyweight tools available to compact catalog controls before the final model-visible filtering step, while the final visible schema still excludes heavyweight tools for local lean models.
What was not tested: full repo check. Testbox-through-Crabbox is unavailable in this environment because Blacksmith auth is missing, so focused remote proof used the repo AWS Crabbox provider.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
